case.title: STADLER v. STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION OF CALIFORNIA

opinion.h1: STADLER v. STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION OF CALIFORNIA, 380 U.S. 252 (1965)
opinion.h4: STADLER ET AL., EXECUTORS v. STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION OF CALIFORNIA. APPEAL FROM THE DISTRICT COURT OF APPEAL OF CALIFORNIA, SECOND APPELLATE DISTRICT. No. 797. Decided March 8, 1965.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Reported below. 227 Cal. App. 2d 314, 38 Cal. Rptr. 587.
Theodore W. Russell and R. Y. Schureman for appellants.
Thomas C. Lynch, Attorney General of California, Dan Kaufman, Assistant Attorney General, and Neal J. Gobar, Deputy Attorney General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
